title: Carnival CEO Arnold Donald's total compensation increased 13% in 2021, and 35% the past 2 years
text: Cruise operator Carnival Corp. CCL, +5.98% CCL, +3.12% disclosed Tuesday that Chief Executive Arnold Donald's total compensation in 2021 was $15.06 million, including $1.50 million in salary, $7.45 million in stock awards and $6.00 million in non-equity incentive plan compensation. Donald's total compensation increased 13.2% from $13.31 million in 2020, which included $857,413 in salary and $12.23 million in stock awards.

title: Why Did Carnival Stock Rally Last Week?
text: Carnival stock, the largest cruise line operator in the U.S., has seen its stock rise by about 10% over the last week. This compares to the broader S&P 500, which declined by almost 2% over the same period, following higher-than-expected U.S. inflation data and the prospect of sizable rate hikes.

title: Cruise stock surge toward the top of the S&P 500's gainers list
text: Cruise stocks surged Monday, and were among the biggest gainers in the S&P 500 SPX, -0.13% , in the wake of Royal Caribbean Group's RCL, +6.97% earnings report in which the cruise operator Chief Executive Jason Liberty said "we are past COVID." Shares of Royal Caribbean's ran up 6.9% in midday trading, Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Ltd.

title: Carnival Share Price Should Easily Cruise Back to $30
text: A warning from the CDC might scare some investors away from CCL stock. Yet, Carnival is apparently buying its own shares with confidence.

title: Carnival cancels Australian cruises due to COVID-19 ban
text: Carnival Cruise Line said it is cancelling planned cruises out of Australia for two of its cruise ships due to the imposed by the Australian government to halt the spread of of COVID-19. Carnival said cruises with Carnival Splendor in the June-September period 2022 and with Carnival Spirit between June and early October have been cancelled and the ships will be moved to America to help with the restart of cruise operations there.

title: Carnival's Outlook Has Greatly Improved, But CCL Stock Remains Very Risky
text: With CCL stock still facing tough, longer term risks, I think there are much better ways to play the reopening trend. Carnival can still be hurt by its high debt load and negative stories about quarantined cruises.

title: Carnival Stock Still Remains 60% Below Pre-Covid Levels. Is There An Opportunity Here?
text: Carnival stock, the largest cruise line operator in the U.S., has declined by almost 2% year-to-date, outperforming the broader S&P 500 which remains down by about 5% over the same period. The outperformance comes as investors likely believe that the worst stages of the Covid-19 pandemic are over.
